title: Trisetum cavanillesii
intro: Trisetum cavanillesii, also known as Spanish wheatgrass or Cavanilles' hair-grass, is a perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. The health benefits of Trisetum cavanillesii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular natural remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Trisetum cavanillesii is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed in traditional medicine to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Trisetum cavanillesii is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Poeae, within the subfamily Pooideae. Further classification places it in the genus Trisetum, which is a group of grasses commonly known as 'browntop' or 'meadow grasses.' The genus Trisetum is part of the family Poaceae, and the order Poales. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Trisetum cavanillesii from other species of grasses.
